contract.name: v2/agency/toptier_code/budgetary_resources.md
contract.description: Returns annual budgetary resources, obligations, outlays, and period breakdowns for the specified toptier agency.

toptier_code.description: CGAC or FREC numeric identifier for the agency whose budgetary resources should be returned.
toptier_code.constraints: Must be a numeric string of length 3 or 4. Unknown numeric codes return a JSON 404 body, non-numeric or wrongly sized segments fall through to an HTML 404, and omitting the trailing slash triggers a 301 redirect to the canonical path.

toptier_code.description: Echo of the requested toptier agency code.

agency_data_by_year.description: Descending fiscal-year entries for the requested agency; observed coverage is FY 2026 back through FY 2017.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year for this entry; responses currently include ten years in descending order.

agency_budgetary_resources.description: Agency-level budgetary resources for the year; null when the agency has not reported figures.

agency_total_obligated.description: Cumulative obligations for the agency through the latest reported period; null when unavailable.

agency_total_outlayed.description: Cumulative outlays for the agency; null when the agency has not reported.

total_budgetary_resources.description: Government-wide total budgetary resources for that fiscal year; identical across agencies for the same year.

agency_obligation_by_period.description: Cumulative obligations by fiscal period (1-12). The array is ascending by period, may be empty when agency totals are null, and the final entry matches `agency_total_obligated` when data exists.

obligated.description: Cumulative obligations reported through this period.

messages.description: Warning or informational strings; observed empty across sampled agencies.

body.detail: Agency with a toptier code of '999' does not exist

quirks: Docs label the response array as `agency_by_year`, but production responses return `agency_data_by_year` ordered newest to oldest.
Contract example shows numbers with comma separators, while the live service returns JSON numbers without formatting.
Additional query parameters such as `fiscal_year` are ignored; responses always include the full year set.
Non-numeric or wrongly sized path segments fall through to an HTML 404 page instead of the JSON 404 used for missing agency codes.
Some agencies report null agency totals and empty obligation arrays for years without submissions, so null must be distinguished from zero.
The cumulative obligation array's largest period matches `agency_total_obligated` when data exists.
Requests without the trailing slash are 301-redirected to the canonical slash form.
risks: Clients expecting JSON errors must handle HTML 404 responses when the path segment is not a numeric 3-4 digit code.
Agency-level budget/resources/outlay fields are frequently null and obligation arrays empty for some agencies, so consumers must handle null values distinctly from zero.
Current fiscal year data can be incomplete, with `agency_obligation_by_period` containing only early periods while totals continue to evolve.
Responses always include multiple fiscal years and could grow large; consumers should be ready to handle sizable payloads.
gaps: Never observed non-empty `messages`; triggers and possible contents remain unknown.
Only production data (FY 2026-2017) was observed; unclear whether the service ever returns a different fiscal range or more than ten years.

probes.notes: pass – baseline; fiscal years run 2026-2017 with partial period coverage for the current year.
pass – shows null totals and empty obligations for years without submissions alongside populated years.
pass – demonstrates a four-digit FREC code and cumulative obligation periods matching the annual total.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Agency with a toptier code of '999' does not exist"}

probes.notes: fail – nonexistent numeric code returns JSON 404 detail.

response.bodyExcerpt:
Not Found

The requested resource was not found on this server.

probes.notes: fail – alpha characters in the path miss the route and return an HTML 404 page.
redirect – missing trailing slash triggers 301 to /api/v2/agency/075/budgetary_resources/.

mismatches: Specification sample uses `agency_by_year`, but the production API returns `agency_data_by_year`.
gaps: Never observed non-empty `messages`; triggers and possible contents remain unknown.
Only production data (FY 2026-2017) was observed; unclear whether the service ever returns a different fiscal range or more than ten years.
risks: Clients expecting JSON errors must handle HTML 404 responses when the path segment is not a numeric 3-4 digit code.
Agency-level budget/resources/outlay fields are frequently null and obligation arrays empty for some agencies, so consumers must handle null values distinctly from zero.
Current fiscal year data can be incomplete, with `agency_obligation_by_period` containing only early periods while totals continue to evolve.
Responses always include multiple fiscal years and could grow large; consumers should be ready to handle sizable payloads.
